proof:
intro S T h
  -- Since the inclusion map maps S to its underlying linear solution, and similarly for T,
  -- if their images are equal, then their underlying linear solutions are equal.
  have h₁ : (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols S : χ.LinSols) = (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols T : χ.LinSols) := h
  -- By the definition of the inclusion map, this means the underlying linear solutions are equal.
  have h₂ : S.toLinSols = T.toLinSols := by simpa [quadSolsInclLinSols] using h₁
  -- Since S and T are quadratic solutions, their underlying linear solutions are equal.
  -- But the underlying linear solution of a quadratic solution uniquely determines it,
  -- because the quadratic condition does not affect the linear part.
  have h₃ : S = T := by
    cases S
    cases T
    -- Simplify the equality of the underlying linear solutions.
    simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
    <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
    (try ring_nf at *) <;>
    (try norm_num at *) <;>
    (try linarith)
  -- Therefore, S and T are equal.
  exact h₃

    -- Use the fact that the linear part is injective

    -- Use the injectivity of the linear part to conclude equality of values
    have h₄ : (S.toLinSols : χ.LinSols).val = (T.toLinSols : χ.LinSols).val := by

    -- Convert back to S and T

  -- Use the equality of values to conclude equality of S and T
  have h₃ : S = T := by
    apply QuadSols.ext
    exact h₂
  -- Conclude the proof
  exact h₃

    simp_all [accsystemlinear.LinSols.val]
